(403/CWW) JAMES GREEN W/PET. THOMAS DOYLE W/RESP. THE PARTIES COUNSEL MET IN CHAMBERS BEFORE GOING ON THE RECORD. RESP IS ADVISED OF HIS RIGHTS AS TO THE CONTEMPT CHARG
ES AGAINST HIM. RESP WAIVES HIS RIGHTS. RESP ADMITS TO 3 COUNTS OF CONTEMPT FOR 2/99, 3/99 AND 4/99. THE COURT PLACES RESP ON INFORMAL PROBATION FOR
PAROLE, OR AT THE DISCRETION OF THE COURT THAT SHALL BE COMMUTED TO 360 HRS OF COMMUNITY SERVICE. THIS SENTENCE IS STAYED. RESP IS ADVISED OF THE
CUTE DOCUMENTS NECESSARY FOR ADDITIONAL WAGE ASSIGNMENT OF $100/WK TO PAY OFF THE ARREARS. ANY LAG TIME UNTIL THE WAGE ASSIGNMENT BEGINS, RESP IS
DINGS AN ADDITIONAL SUM OF $500, ADDED TO PREVIOUSLY ORDERED A/F OF $1000, FOR A TOTAL A/F OF $1500 PAYABLE AT THE RATE OF $100/MO COMMENCING 11/17/99

(403/MAS) JAMES GREEN WITH PET. THOMAS DOYLE WITH RESP. STIP FOR PRO TEM SIGNED. RESP WISHED TO DISMISS HIS COUNSEL AND MR. DOYLE MADE REQUEST TO BE EXCUSED AS ATTORNEY
OF RECORD FOR RESP. COURT WANTED RESP TO BE SURE OF HIS DECISION GIVEN ISSUES OF CASE. AFTER BRIEF RECESS, PARTIES RETURNED TO COURT AND MR. DOYLE
EN APPROPRIATE. PET AND RESP SWORN AND TESTIFIED. AFTER ARGUMENT AND SUBMISSION, COURT MAKES FINDINGS AND ORDERS THAT ARE STATED INTO THE RECORD (RE:
O COURT BY 6/2/00. PARTIES AND ATTORNEYS SHALL MEET WITH COURT ON 6/7/00 @2:00PM FOR RESOLUTION OF SAID MATTER, HOPEFULLY BY WAY OF A CHAMBERS
IS TIME AND ADMONISHES RESP TO ABIDE BY ORDER. BY NEXT HEARING, RESP TO PROVIDE PROOF THAT HE PAID EACH AND EVERY CLAIM OF NONPAYMENT TO PET. COURT TO
OIR DIRED AS TO JURISDICTIONAL FACTS BY HER ATTORNEY DURING TESTIMONY. COURT ENTERS JUDGMENT OF DISSOLUTION AS OF TODAY'S DATE. PET TO PREPARE ORDER.
COURT WANTS ORDER AFTER HEARING BY 6/2/00. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY RICHARD BOTTO #5680 MENT OF DISSOLUTION AS OF TODAY'S DATE. PET TO PREPARE ORDER.
STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE TEMPORARY JUDGE -MARJORIE A. SLABACH
